title: EDL generation and validation
analysis:
This task creates two new modules in autopilot/plan/: edl.py for Claude Opus tool-use EDL construction and validator.py for automated EDL validation. The pattern closely follows autopilot/plan/script.py (task 16) which already handles LLM calls via the anthropic SDK, loads system prompts from autopilot/prompts/, uses CatalogDB for persistence, and updates narrative status. Key differences: (1) edl.py uses tool-use/function-calling instead of plain text responses — the LLM returns tool_use content blocks which must be collected into a JSON EDL structure, (2) validator.py provides pure-logic validation (no LLM), and (3) there's a retry loop: if validation fails, re-prompt with error feedback up to 3 times. The edit_planner.md prompt already exists with 8 tool definitions. DB already has edit_plans table with upsert_edit_plan/get_edit_plan methods. The existing conftest.py provides catalog_db (in-memory SQLite) and prompts_dir fixtures. The anthropic SDK mock pattern from test_script.py can be adapted for tool-use responses (content blocks with type='tool_use' instead of type='text').

REVIEW FEEDBACK (post-step-24): All _check_* helpers in validator.py use bare dict subscript access (clip["in_timecode"]) and call timecode_to_seconds() without catching ValueError/KeyError. Since the LLM can produce any format, malformed timecodes or missing keys cause unhandled exceptions that propagate through validate_edl into generate_edl's retry loop, breaking the API contract (caller gets ValueError/KeyError instead of EdlError). Additionally, float(raw_duration) in _check_timecode_bounds can raise on non-numeric catalog data. Steps 25-28 add TDD pairs to fix all six identified issues.

prerequisites.description: Create empty autopilot/plan/edl.py and autopilot/plan/validator.py files with module docstrings and __all__ stubs so imports work

steps.description: Write tests for ValidationResult dataclass and validate_edl public API surface: importability, signature checks, ValidationResult has passed/errors/warnings fields

Implement ValidationResult dataclass in validator.py with passed: bool, errors: list[str], warnings: list[str] fields. Implement validate_edl stub that returns a passing ValidationResult. Export EdlValidationError, ValidationResult, validate_edl in __all__.

Write tests for validate_edl: no overlapping clips on same track. Test cases: (a) non-overlapping clips pass, (b) overlapping clips on same track fail with descriptive error, (c) overlapping clips on different tracks pass
Implement overlap detection in validate_edl: group select_clip entries by track, sort by in_timecode, check for overlaps. Add timecode_to_seconds helper to parse HH:MM:SS.mmm format.

Write tests for validate_edl: total duration check within ±10% of scripted target. Test cases: (a) within tolerance passes, (b) over 10% above fails, (c) over 10% below fails, (d) exactly at boundary passes
Implement total duration check in validate_edl: sum durations of all select_clip entries on track 1, compare against target_duration_seconds in EDL, flag error if outside ±10%
Write tests for validate_edl: all referenced clip_ids exist in catalog. Test cases: (a) all valid clip_ids pass, (b) unknown clip_id fails with error listing the missing ID
Implement clip_id existence check: collect all clip_ids from select_clip entries, verify each exists via db.get_media(), report errors for missing ones

Write tests for validate_edl: in/out points within clip duration bounds. Test cases: (a) valid in/out within duration passes, (b) out_timecode exceeding clip duration fails, (c) in_timecode after out_timecode fails
Implement in/out timecode bounds check: for each select_clip, convert timecodes to seconds, verify both are within [0, clip_duration] and in < out
Write tests for validate_edl: audio levels broadcast-safe range (-24 to 0 dBFS). Test cases: (a) levels in range pass, (b) level below -24 dB generates warning, (c) level above 0 dB generates error
Implement audio level check: iterate set_audio entries, verify level_db in [-24, 0] for speech clips. Values outside range produce errors; optionally issue warnings for borderline values.
Write tests for generate_edl public API surface: importability, signature (narrative_id, db, config) -> dict, EdlError is a proper Exception subclass
Implement EdlError exception class and generate_edl function stub in edl.py that raises EdlError('Not implemented'). Export EdlError, generate_edl, TOOL_DEFINITIONS in __all__.

Write tests for generate_edl: LLM interaction basics. Test cases: (a) calls anthropic with config.planning_model, (b) passes edit_planner.md as system prompt, (c) passes tool definitions in tools param, (d) user message includes script data and storyboard, (e) narrative not found raises EdlError

Implement generate_edl LLM call: load script from narrative_scripts table, load storyboard via build_narrative_storyboard, load edit_planner.md prompt, build TOOL_DEFINITIONS list from prompt JSON blocks, call anthropic with tools= parameter, handle tool_use content blocks by collecting tool calls into EDL structure

Write tests for generate_edl: tool-use response collection. Mock anthropic to return content blocks with type='tool_use', verify (a) select_clip calls collected into clips array, (b) add_transition calls collected into transitions array, (c) set_audio calls collected into audio_settings array, (d) all 8 tool types properly categorized in EDL dict

Implement tool call collection: iterate response.content blocks, dispatch by tool name to appropriate EDL section (clips, transitions, crop_modes, titles, audio_settings, music, voiceovers, broll_requests). Include target_duration_seconds from narrative.

Write tests for generate_edl: DB persistence and status update. Test cases: (a) EDL stored in edit_plans table via upsert_edit_plan, (b) narrative status updated to 'planned', (c) validation result also stored in edit_plans.validation_json
Implement DB persistence: after collecting EDL, call validate_edl, store edl_json and validation_json via db.upsert_edit_plan, update narrative status to 'planned' via db.update_narrative_status

Write tests for generate_edl: validation retry loop. Test cases: (a) validation passes on first try — no retry, (b) validation fails then passes on retry — LLM called twice with error feedback in second call, (c) validation fails 3 times — EdlError raised with validation errors, (d) retry message includes previous validation errors

Implement retry loop: after LLM call, run validate_edl. If validation fails, re-prompt LLM with error list (up to 3 retries). On final failure, raise EdlError with concatenated validation errors. On success, store and return EDL.

Write integration test: full pipeline with seeded DB data -> generate_edl with mocked LLM returning valid tool-use calls -> verify EDL structure, DB state (edit_plans stored, narrative status 'planned'), validation passes
Final cleanup: ensure all error paths are covered, add logging, verify all __all__ exports are correct, run full test suite

Write tests for validate_edl defensive handling of malformed timecodes and missing keys. Test class TestMalformedTimecodes with cases: (a) clip with malformed timecode string (e.g. 'not-a-timecode') returns ValidationResult with passed=False and descriptive error, never raises ValueError; (b) clip with missing in_timecode key returns ValidationResult with error, never raises KeyError; (c) clip with missing out_timecode key returns ValidationResult with error, never raises KeyError; (d) clip with non-standard format (e.g. '10s') returns error in result; (e) EDL with mix of valid and malformed clips collects errors for bad clips but still validates the good ones (overlap/duration checks run on parseable clips only). All tests assert validate_edl does NOT raise — it always returns ValidationResult.

Fix _check_overlaps, _check_duration, and _check_timecode_bounds in validator.py to wrap all timecode access and parsing in try/except (KeyError, ValueError). In _check_overlaps: wrap the sorted() key lambda and loop body — on exception, append descriptive error like 'Clip {clip_id}: invalid or missing timecode in overlap check' and skip the clip. In _check_duration: wrap clip['in_timecode']/clip['out_timecode'] access and timecode_to_seconds calls — on exception, append error and skip clip from duration sum. In _check_timecode_bounds: wrap clip['in_timecode']/clip['out_timecode'] access and timecode_to_seconds calls — on exception, append 'Clip {clip_id}: invalid or missing timecode' and continue to next clip. This ensures validate_edl never raises unexpectedly and all failures flow through ValidationResult.errors, maintaining the API contract with generate_edl's retry loop.

Write tests for validate_edl defensive handling of non-numeric duration_seconds in catalog data. Test class TestMalformedCatalogData with cases: (a) db.get_media returns {'duration_seconds': None} — validate_edl returns ValidationResult with error about non-numeric duration, never raises TypeError; (b) db.get_media returns {'duration_seconds': 'bad'} — returns error, never raises ValueError; (c) db.get_media returns {} (missing duration_seconds key) — returns error or gracefully handles with default. All tests assert validate_edl does NOT raise.

Fix _check_timecode_bounds in validator.py to wrap float(raw_duration) in try/except (ValueError, TypeError). On exception, append 'Clip {clip_id}: non-numeric duration_seconds in catalog' to errors and continue to next clip. This handles None, non-numeric strings, and other unexpected types from db.get_media() without crashing.

design_decisions.decision: EDL structure is a flat dict with typed arrays for each tool category (clips, transitions, crop_modes, titles, audio_settings, music, voiceovers, broll_requests) plus target_duration_seconds
design_decisions.rationale: Each tool type maps cleanly to a list. This makes validation straightforward (iterate clips for overlap check, iterate audio_settings for level check, etc.) and is directly serializable as JSON for edit_plans.edl_json.

design_decisions.decision: Use tool-use content block parsing (type='tool_use', name, input) from the Anthropic API rather than custom JSON parsing
design_decisions.rationale: The Anthropic SDK returns structured tool_use blocks when tools are provided. This is more reliable than parsing free-text JSON and is the idiomatic approach for tool-use workflows.

design_decisions.decision: validate_edl takes the EDL dict + CatalogDB (for clip existence/duration checks) and returns a ValidationResult dataclass rather than raising exceptions
design_decisions.rationale: Validation needs to report multiple errors/warnings at once. A result object allows collecting all issues in one pass, which is essential for the retry feedback loop (send ALL errors to the LLM, not just the first one).

design_decisions.decision: Timecode helper (timecode_to_seconds) is implemented as a module-level utility in validator.py rather than a separate utils module
design_decisions.rationale: Only edl.py and validator.py need timecode conversion. Keeping it local avoids adding a utils module for one function. Can be promoted later if more modules need it.

design_decisions.decision: Audio level validation uses -24 to 0 dBFS range with values outside producing errors, not just warnings
design_decisions.rationale: The task spec says 'broadcast-safe ranges (-24 to 0 dBFS)' — values outside this range could cause clipping or inaudible content. Making these errors ensures the retry loop corrects them.

design_decisions.decision: TOOL_DEFINITIONS are parsed from edit_planner.md JSON blocks at module load time rather than hardcoded in Python
design_decisions.rationale: The prompt already contains the authoritative tool definitions. Parsing them ensures the code and prompt stay in sync. Also mirrors how test_prompt_edit_planner.py validates the prompt.

design_decisions.decision: Retry loop maximum is 3 attempts (1 initial + up to 3 retries = 4 total LLM calls maximum, or 1 initial + 2 retries = 3 total per task description 'up to 3 retries')
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec says 'up to 3 retries'. We interpret this as 3 additional attempts after the first failure, for a maximum of 4 LLM calls total. This balances cost vs. success rate.

design_decisions.decision: All _check_* helpers must catch KeyError and ValueError from timecode access/parsing and append errors to the list instead of propagating exceptions
design_decisions.rationale: validate_edl's contract is to NEVER raise unexpectedly — it returns ValidationResult. Since LLM output is untrusted, any timecode field could be missing or malformed. Uncaught exceptions break generate_edl's retry loop (caller gets ValueError/KeyError instead of EdlError), bypassing the retry mechanism entirely. Defensive try/except in each helper ensures all failures are surfaced as validation errors that trigger retries.

design_decisions.decision: float(raw_duration) for catalog duration_seconds must be wrapped in try/except
design_decisions.rationale: Catalog data accessed via db.get_media() could return None or a non-numeric string for duration_seconds (schema mismatch, legacy data, or missing field). An unguarded float() call would raise TypeError/ValueError, breaking the validate_edl contract. Wrapping it ensures graceful degradation.

reuse.what: CatalogDB with upsert_edit_plan, get_edit_plan, get_media, get_narrative, get_narrative_script, update_narrative_status
reuse.how: All DB CRUD methods needed for edl.py already exist. edl.py stores results via upsert_edit_plan and updates narrative status. validator.py uses get_media for clip existence/duration checks.
generate_edl calls build_narrative_storyboard(narrative_id, db) to get the storyboard text that is included in the LLM user message, same as generate_script does.
generate_edl uses config.planning_model to select the Claude Opus model for the API call.

reuse.what: edit_planner.md prompt with 8 tool JSON definitions
reuse.how: Loaded as system prompt. JSON blocks are parsed to extract TOOL_DEFINITIONS for the anthropic tools= parameter.

reuse.what: conftest.py fixtures (catalog_db, prompts_dir) and extract_json_blocks helper
reuse.how: catalog_db provides in-memory SQLite for all tests. prompts_dir used if needed. extract_json_blocks could help parse tool defs in tests.
Adapted for tool-use responses: instead of mock content with .text, mock content blocks with type='tool_use', name=tool_name, input=params_dict. Same patch.dict(sys.modules) pattern.
Follow same pattern: EdlError for edl.py, EdlValidationError for validator.py (or just use EdlError for both since validator doesn't raise, it returns ValidationResult).
Can reuse the same DB seeding pattern for EDL tests — need narrative with script data, media files with durations for validation.
The new malformed-input tests in steps 25/27 can reuse _mock_db() for DB mocking and adapt _make_bounded_edl() patterns for creating EDL fixtures with bad timecodes or missing keys.
